content.explanation:
**Buildings, Worms & Insects Vocabulary:**

**BUILDINGS & STRUCTURES:**
| English | Hindi |
|---------|-------|
| Skyscraper | ऊँची इमारत |
| Bungalow | बंगला |
| Cottage | छोटा घर/कुटिया |
| Mansion | हवेली/बड़ा महल |
| Warehouse | गोदाम |
| Courtyard | आँगन |
| Balcony | बालकनी |
| Basement | तहखाना |
| Corridor | गलियारा |
| Staircase | सीढ़ियाँ |

**INSECTS (कीड़े):**
| English | Hindi |
|---------|-------|
| Butterfly | तितली |
| Bee | मधुमक्खी |
| Wasp | ततैया |
| Ant | चींटी |
| Cockroach | तिलचट्टा |
| Mosquito | मच्छर |
| Beetle | गुबरैला |
| Grasshopper | टिड्डी |
| Firefly | जुगनू |
| Dragonfly | व्याध-पतंग |

**WORMS & CRAWLIES:**
Earthworm (केंचुआ), Caterpillar (इल्ली), Centipede (सौ-पाँव), Snail (घोंघा), Slug (बिना खोल का घोंघा)
rules: Building names: use "a/an" for singular: "a skyscraper", "a bungalow"
"There is a..." / "There are many..." for describing buildings
Insects are countable: an ant, two butterflies, several mosquitoes
Some insects are uncountable when referring to infestations: "There is mosquito problem"
Describing buildings: "The building has X floors." / "It is a X-storey building."
Insect actions: bees sting, ants crawl, butterflies flutter, mosquitoes bite
Prepositions with buildings: on the ground floor, in the basement, on the balcony
content.memoryTrick:
**Building = Structure where people live/work**
🏗️ Sky → Skyscraper (touches the sky)
🏡 Bungalow = ground floor only
🏚️ Cottage = small, cozy, country-style

🐝 Bee = Makes honey, lives in hive
🦋 Butterfly = starts as caterpillar
🦟 Mosquito = bites, spreads disease

examples.hindi: उस शहर में कई ऊँची इमारतें हैं।
examples.english: There are many skyscrapers in that city.
The goods are stored in the warehouse.

examples.hindi: घर के आँगन में एक पेड़ है।
examples.english: There is a tree in the courtyard.
A caterpillar transforms into a butterfly.

mistakes.why: 'Floors' is a countable noun — use plural
Adjective comes BEFORE the noun in English
'Problem' is singular — 'mosquito' here is used as adjective modifier

speakingTips: Describing your home: 'I live in a two-storey bungalow with a courtyard.'
Nature conversations: 'Have you ever seen a firefly? They glow in the dark!'
Environmental: 'Bees are essential for pollination — we must protect them.'

vocabulary.example: There is a cockroach infestation in the kitchen.
The facade of the building is stunning.
Mosquito larvae grow in stagnant water.
We called a pest control to exterminate the insects.

practice.hindi: वह पुरानी इमारत टूट रही है।
alternatives: the old structure is falling apart.

practice.english: There is a spider in the classroom.
alternatives: a spider has entered the classroom.
ants are extremely industrious.

practice.english: The mosquito is a dangerous insect.
alternatives: mosquitoes are very harmful insects.

practice.hindi: वह स्कूल की इमारत बहुत पुरानी है।
practice.english: That school building is very old.
alternatives: the school structure is very ancient.
a temple is located here.

practice.hindi: पुस्तकालय शहर के बीच में है।
practice.english: The library is in the middle of the city.
alternatives: the library is in the city centre.
butterflies are very pretty.

practice.hindi: गर्मी में मक्खियाँ बहुत परेशान करती हैं।
practice.english: Flies are very troublesome in summer.
alternatives: flies are a nuisance in hot weather.
this is a very big hospital.
he started a new store.

practice.english: The cockroach comes out at night.
alternatives: cockroaches are active at night.
bees produce honey.

practice.hindi: स्टेशन के पास एक होटल है।
practice.english: There is a hotel near the station.
alternatives: a hotel is located near the station.

practice.hindi: पार्क के बगल में एक दुकान है।
practice.english: There is a shop next to the park.
alternatives: a store is situated beside the park.
